items.query: How is the exact sparsity of a vector in R^n measured?
items.reference_answer: It is measured by the size of the vector's support, meaning the number of indices whose coefficients are nonzero. This quantity is denoted by ||x||_0.

items.query: Why might an audio signal be considered sparse even when the signal itself is not sparse in the time domain?
items.reference_answer: An audio signal may become sparse after changing to the frequency basis: although its time-domain values are generally nonzero, its Fourier transform can have significant values concentrated in a relatively small set of frequencies.
